title: Radiant Argentinian Barley Pancakes
description: Malty, fluffy, light Argentinian pancakes with paprika and feta, perfect for a Mediterranean-friendly breakfast.
instructions: In a large bowl whisk together barley flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, salt, and paprika.
In a separate bowl beat the egg, then add water and olive oil, whisk until combined.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, leaving a few lumps for fluffiness.
Fold in crumbled feta and chopped parsley until evenly distributed.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and brush with a light layer of olive oil.
Pour 1/4 cup batter for each pancake, cooking until bubbles form on the surface, about 2 minutes.
Flip the pancakes and cook for another 1-2 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Serve hot with a drizzle of olive oil or a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
